audio.c(3) Library Functions Manual audio.c(3)

NAME

audio.c - 🔊 Client audio management: capture thread, sample processing, and playback coordination

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct audio_send_packet_t
Audio packet for async sending.

Macros


#define AUDIO_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE 32
#define AUDIO_VOLUME_BOOST 1.0f
#define OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES 960
#define OPUS_MAX_PACKET_SIZE 500
#define CAPTURE_READ_SIZE (OPUS_FRAME_SAMPLES * 4)
#define MAX_BATCH_FRAMES 8
#define BATCH_TIMEOUT_NS (40LL * NS_PER_MS_INT)

Functions


void audio_sender_init (void)
Initialize async audio sender queue and thread. void audio_sender_cleanup (void)
Cleanup async audio sender. void audio_process_received_samples (const float *samples, int num_samples)
Process received audio samples from server. int audio_client_init ()
Initialize audio subsystem. int audio_start_thread ()
Start audio capture thread. void audio_stop_thread ()
Stop audio capture thread. bool audio_thread_exited ()
Check if audio capture thread has exited. void audio_cleanup ()
Cleanup audio subsystem. client_audio_pipeline_t * audio_get_pipeline (void)
Get the audio pipeline (for advanced usage). int audio_decode_opus (const uint8_t *opus_data, size_t opus_len, float *output, int max_samples)
Decode Opus packet using the audio pipeline. audio_context_t * audio_get_context (void)
Get the global audio context for use by other subsystems.

Detailed Description

🔊 Client audio management: capture thread, sample processing, and playback coordination

The audio system implements a dual-thread architecture:

  • Capture Thread: Records microphone input and transmits to server
  • Playback System: Receives server audio and plays through speakers
  • Processing Pipeline: Real-time audio enhancement and filtering
  • Coordination: Thread-safe communication between capture and playback

Audio Processing Pipeline

Both incoming and outgoing audio undergo comprehensive processing:

1.
High-pass Filter: Removes low-frequency rumble and noise
2.
Noise Gate: Eliminates background noise during silence
3.
Dynamic Range: Soft clipping to prevent harsh distortion
4.
Volume Control: Configurable boost for optimal listening levels
5.
Batching: Groups samples for efficient network transmission

Capture Thread Management

Audio capture runs in a dedicated thread:

  • Continuous Recording: Real-time microphone sample capture
  • Processing Chain: Applies filters and enhancement algorithms
  • Network Transmission: Sends processed samples to server
  • Adaptive Quality: Noise gate reduces traffic during silence
  • Thread Safety: Coordinated shutdown and resource management

Batching and Network Efficiency

Audio samples are batched for network efficiency:

  • Batch Accumulation: Collect multiple sample packets
  • Smart Transmission: Send batches when full or gate closes
  • Reduced Overhead: Fewer network packets for better performance
  • Quality Preservation: Maintain audio quality while optimizing bandwidth

Platform Audio Integration

Uses PortAudio for cross-platform audio support:

  • Device Enumeration: Automatic microphone and speaker detection
  • Format Negotiation: Optimal sample rate and bit depth selection
  • Low Latency: Optimized for real-time audio processing
  • Error Handling: Graceful handling of device conflicts and changes

Integration Points

  • main.c: Audio subsystem lifecycle and initialization
  • server.c: Audio packet transmission to server
  • protocol.c: Incoming audio packet processing and dispatch
  • lib/audio.c: Low-level PortAudio device management
  • lib/mixer.c: Audio filtering and processing algorithms

Error Handling

Audio errors handled with graceful degradation:

  • Device Unavailable: Continue without audio, log warnings
  • Processing Errors: Skip problematic samples, maintain stream
  • Network Errors: Continue processing, let connection management handle
  • Resource Errors: Clean shutdown with proper resource release

Resource Management

Careful audio resource lifecycle:

  • Context Management: Proper PortAudio context initialization/cleanup
  • Thread Coordination: Clean thread shutdown and resource release
  • Buffer Management: Efficient sample buffer allocation and reuse
  • Device Release: Proper microphone and speaker device cleanup

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Definition in file src/client/audio.c.

Function Documentation

void audio_cleanup (void )

Cleanup audio subsystem. Cleanup audio subsystem

Stops audio threads and cleans up PortAudio resources. Called during client shutdown.

Definition at line 1236 of file src/client/audio.c.

1236                      {
1237   if (!GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
1238     return;
1239   }
1240 
1241   // Stop capture thread first (stops producing packets)
1242   audio_stop_thread();
1243 
1244   // Stop async sender thread (drains queue and exits)
1245   audio_sender_cleanup();
1246 
1247   // Terminate PortAudio FIRST to properly free device resources before cleanup
1248   // This must happen before audio_stop_duplex() and audio_destroy()
1249   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
1250 
1251   // Stop audio stream before destroying pipeline to prevent race condition.
1252   // PortAudio may invoke the callback one more time after we request stop.
1253   // We need to clear the pipeline pointer first so the callback can't access freed memory.
1254   if (g_audio_context.initialized) {
1255     audio_stop_duplex(&g_audio_context);
1256   }
1257 
1258   // Clear the pipeline pointer from audio context BEFORE destroying pipeline
1259   // This prevents any lingering PortAudio callbacks from trying to access freed memory
1260   audio_set_pipeline(&g_audio_context, NULL);
1261 
1262   // Sleep to allow CoreAudio threads to finish executing callbacks.
1263   // On macOS, CoreAudio's internal threads may continue running after Pa_StopStream() returns.
1264   // The duplex_callback may still be in-flight on other threads. Even after we set the pipeline
1265   // pointer to NULL, a CoreAudio thread may have already cached the pointer before the assignment.
1266   // This sleep ensures all in-flight callbacks have fully completed before we destroy the pipeline.
1267   // WORKAROUND: Skip sleep during shutdown (process is exiting anyway)
1268   // 500ms is sufficient on macOS for CoreAudio's internal thread pool to completely wind down.
1269   // platform_sleep_us(500 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 500ms - macOS CoreAudio needs time to shut down all threads
1270 
1271   // Destroy audio pipeline (handles Opus, AEC, etc.)
1272   if (g_audio_pipeline) {
1273     client_audio_pipeline_destroy(g_audio_pipeline);
1274     g_audio_pipeline = NULL;
1275     log_debug("Audio pipeline destroyed");
1276   }
1277 
1278   // Close WAV dumpers
1279   if (g_wav_capture_raw) {
1280     wav_writer_close(g_wav_capture_raw);
1281     g_wav_capture_raw = NULL;
1282     log_debug("Closed audio capture raw dump");
1283   }
1284   if (g_wav_capture_processed) {
1285     wav_writer_close(g_wav_capture_processed);
1286     g_wav_capture_processed = NULL;
1287     log_debug("Closed audio capture processed dump");
1288   }
1289   if (g_wav_playback_received) {
1290     wav_writer_close(g_wav_playback_received);
1291     g_wav_playback_received = NULL;
1292     log_debug("Closed audio playback received dump");
1293   }
1294 
1295   // Finally destroy the audio context
1296   if (g_audio_context.initialized) {
1297     audio_destroy(&g_audio_context);
1298   }
1299 }




References audio_destroy(), audio_sender_cleanup(),
    audio_set_pipeline(), audio_stop_duplex(),
    audio_stop_thread(), audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    client_audio_pipeline_destroy(), GET_OPTION, log_debug,
    and wav_writer_close().






int
  audio_client_init (void )


Initialize audio subsystem. Initialize audio subsystem




Sets up PortAudio context, creates the audio pipeline, and starts
    audio playback/capture if audio is enabled.




Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 931 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


931                         {
932   if (!GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
933     return 0; // Audio disabled - not an error
934   }
935 
936   // Use lifecycle to ensure only one thread initializes audio at a time
937   // This prevents race conditions in worker thread creation
938   if (!lifecycle_init_once(&g_audio_client_init_lc)) {
939     // Another thread is already initializing, wait for it to complete
940     while (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_audio_client_init_lc)) {
941       platform_sleep_us(1000); // 1ms
942     }
943     return 0; // Already initialized by another thread
944   }
945 
946   // Clean up previous audio context if it was already initialized
947   // This prevents leaks and thread creation issues on reconnection
948   if (g_audio_context.initialized) {
949     log_debug("Cleaning up previous audio context before reinitializing");
950     audio_destroy(&g_audio_context);
951     // NOTE: Do NOT reset lifecycle - keep it INITIALIZED so only ONE global
952     // audio context is ever created. Subsequent calls will return early.
953   }
954 
955   // Initialize WAV dumper for received audio if debugging enabled
956   if (wav_dump_enabled()) {
957     g_wav_playback_received = wav_writer_open("/tmp/audio_playback_received.wav", AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, 1);
958     if (g_wav_playback_received) {
959       log_debug("Audio debugging enabled: dumping received audio to /tmp/audio_playback_received.wav");
960     }
961   }
962 
963   // Initialize PortAudio context using library function
964   log_debug("DEBUG: About to call audio_init()...");
965   if (audio_init(&g_audio_context) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
966     log_error("Failed to initialize audio system");
967     // Clean up WAV writer if it was opened
968     if (g_wav_playback_received) {
969       wav_writer_close(g_wav_playback_received);
970       g_wav_playback_received = NULL;
971     }
972     lifecycle_init_abort(&g_audio_client_init_lc);
973     return -1;
974   }
975   log_debug("DEBUG: audio_init() completed successfully");
976 
977   lifecycle_init_commit(&g_audio_client_init_lc);
978 
979   // Create unified audio pipeline (handles AEC, AGC, noise suppression, Opus)
980   client_audio_pipeline_config_t pipeline_config = client_audio_pipeline_default_config();
981   pipeline_config.opus_bitrate = 128000; // 128 kbps AUDIO mode for music quality
982 
983   // Enable echo cancellation, AGC, and essential processing for clear audio
984   // Noise suppression and VAD can destroy music quality, so keep them disabled
985   pipeline_config.flags.echo_cancel = true;     // ENABLE: removes echo
986   pipeline_config.flags.jitter_buffer = true;   // ENABLE: needed for AEC sync
987   pipeline_config.flags.noise_suppress = false; // DISABLED: destroys music quality
988   pipeline_config.flags.agc = true;             // ENABLE: boost quiet microphones (35 dB gain)
989   pipeline_config.flags.vad = false;            // DISABLED: destroys music quality
990   pipeline_config.flags.compressor = true;      // ENABLE: prevent clipping from AGC boost
991   pipeline_config.flags.noise_gate = false;     // DISABLED: would cut quiet music passages
992   pipeline_config.flags.highpass = true;        // ENABLE: remove rumble and low-frequency feedback
993   pipeline_config.flags.lowpass = false;        // DISABLED: preserve high-frequency content
994 
995   // Set jitter buffer margin for smooth playback without excessive delay
996   // 100ms is conservative - AEC3 will adapt to actual network delay automatically
997   // We don't tune this; let the system adapt to its actual conditions
998   pipeline_config.jitter_margin_ns = 100;
999 
1000   log_debug("DEBUG: About to create audio pipeline...");
1001   g_audio_pipeline = client_audio_pipeline_create(&pipeline_config);
1002   log_debug("DEBUG: client_audio_pipeline_create() returned");
1003   if (!g_audio_pipeline) {
1004     log_error("Failed to create audio pipeline");
1005     audio_destroy(&g_audio_context);
1006     // Clean up WAV writer if it was opened
1007     if (g_wav_playback_received) {
1008       wav_writer_close(g_wav_playback_received);
1009       g_wav_playback_received = NULL;
1010     }
1011     return -1;
1012   }
1013 
1014   log_debug("Audio pipeline created: %d Hz sample rate, %d bps bitrate", pipeline_config.sample_rate,
1015             pipeline_config.opus_bitrate);
1016 
1017   // Associate pipeline with audio context for echo cancellation
1018   // The audio output callback will feed playback samples directly to AEC3 from the speaker output,
1019   // ensuring proper timing synchronization (not from the decode path 50-100ms earlier)
1020   audio_set_pipeline(&g_audio_context, (void *)g_audio_pipeline);
1021 
1022   // Start full-duplex audio (simultaneous capture + playback for perfect AEC3 timing)
1023   if (audio_start_duplex(&g_audio_context) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1024     log_error("Failed to start full-duplex audio");
1025     client_audio_pipeline_destroy(g_audio_pipeline);
1026     g_audio_pipeline = NULL;
1027     audio_destroy(&g_audio_context);
1028     // Clean up WAV writer if it was opened
1029     if (g_wav_playback_received) {
1030       wav_writer_close(g_wav_playback_received);
1031       g_wav_playback_received = NULL;
1032     }
1033     return -1;
1034   }
1035 
1036   // Note: audio_sender_init() is now called from protocol_start_connection()
1037   // after connection succeeds, to prevent deadlock if connection fails
1038 
1039   return 0;
1040 }






References client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::agc,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, audio_destroy(), audio_init(),
    AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE, audio_set_pipeline(),
    audio_start_duplex(), client_audio_pipeline_create(),
    client_audio_pipeline_default_config(),
    client_audio_pipeline_destroy(),
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::compressor,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::echo_cancel,
    client_audio_pipeline_config_t::flags, GET_OPTION,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::highpass,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::jitter_buffer,
    client_audio_pipeline_config_t::jitter_margin_ns,
    lifecycle_init_abort(), lifecycle_init_commit(),
    lifecycle_init_once(), lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    log_debug, log_error,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::lowpass,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::noise_gate,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::noise_suppress,
    client_audio_pipeline_config_t::opus_bitrate,
    platform_sleep_us(),
    client_audio_pipeline_config_t::sample_rate,
    client_audio_pipeline_flags_t::vad, wav_dump_enabled(),
    wav_writer_close(), and wav_writer_open().






int
  audio_decode_opus (const uint8_t * opus_data, size_t opus_len, float *
  output, int max_samples)


Decode Opus packet using the audio pipeline.


Parameters


opus_data Opus packet data


opus_len Opus packet length


output Output buffer for decoded samples


max_samples Maximum samples output buffer can hold


Returns


Number of decoded samples, or negative on error

  src/client/audio.c.


1321                                                                                                  {
1322   if (!g_audio_pipeline || !output || max_samples <= 0) {
1323     return -1;
1324   }
1325 
1326   return client_audio_pipeline_playback(g_audio_pipeline, opus_data, (int)opus_len, output, max_samples);
1327 }

void
  audio_process_received_samples (const float * samples, int
  num_samples)


Process received audio samples from server. Process received audio
    samples from server




Uses the audio pipeline for processing:



  
1.

  
Input validation and size checking

  
2.

  
Feed samples to pipeline (applies soft clipping)

  
3.

  
Feed echo reference for AEC

  
4.

  
Submit processed samples to PortAudio playback queue




Parameters


samples Raw audio sample data from server


num_samples Number of samples in the buffer


Definition at line 431 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


431                                                                            {
432   // Validate parameters
433   if (!samples || num_samples <= 0) {
434     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid audio samples: samples=%p, num_samples=%d", (void *)samples, num_samples);
435     return;
436   }
437 
438   if (!GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
439     log_warn_every(NS_PER_MS_INT, "Received audio samples but audio is disabled");
440     return;
441   }
442 
443   // Allow both single packets and batched packets
444   if (num_samples > AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES) {
445     log_warn("Audio packet too large: %d samples (max %d)", num_samples, AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES);
446     return;
447   }
448 
449   // Calculate RMS energy of received samples
450   float sum_squares = 0.0f;
451   for (int i = 0; i < num_samples; i++) {
452     sum_squares += samples[i] * samples[i];
453   }
454   float received_rms = sqrtf(sum_squares / num_samples);
455 
456   // DUMP: Received audio from server (before playback processing)
457   if (g_wav_playback_received) {
458     wav_writer_write(g_wav_playback_received, samples, num_samples);
459   }
460 
461   // Track samples for analysis
462   if (GET_OPTION(audio_analysis_enabled)) {
463     for (int i = 0; i < num_samples; i++) {
464       audio_analysis_track_received_sample(samples[i]);
465     }
466   }
467 
468   // Copy samples to playback buffer (no processing needed - mixer already handled clipping)
469   float audio_buffer[AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES];
470   memcpy(audio_buffer, samples, (size_t)num_samples * sizeof(float));
471 
472   // DEBUG: Log what we're writing to playback buffer (with first 4 samples to verify audio integrity)
473   static int recv_count = 0;
474   recv_count++;
475   if (recv_count <= 10 || recv_count % 50 == 0) {
476     float peak = 0.0f;
477     for (int i = 0; i < num_samples; i++) {
478       float abs_val = fabsf(samples[i]);
479       if (abs_val > peak)
480         peak = abs_val;
481     }
482     log_debug("CLIENT AUDIO RECV #%d: %d samples, RMS=%.6f, Peak=%.6f, first4=[%.4f,%.4f,%.4f,%.4f]", recv_count,
483               num_samples, received_rms, peak, num_samples > 0 ? samples[0] : 0.0f, num_samples > 1 ? samples[1] : 0.0f,
484               num_samples > 2 ? samples[2] : 0.0f, num_samples > 3 ? samples[3] : 0.0f);
485   }
486 
487   // Submit to playback system (goes to jitter buffer and speakers)
488   // NOTE: AEC3's AnalyzeRender is called in output_callback() when audio actually plays,
489   // NOT here. The jitter buffer adds 50-100ms delay, so calling AnalyzeRender here
490   // would give AEC3 the wrong timing and break echo cancellation.
491   audio_write_samples(&g_audio_context, audio_buffer, num_samples);
492 
493   // Log latency after writing to playback buffer
494   if (g_audio_context.playback_buffer) {
495     size_t buffer_samples = audio_ring_buffer_available_read(g_audio_context.playback_buffer);
496     float buffer_latency_ms = (float)buffer_samples / 48.0f;
497     log_dev_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "LATENCY: Client playback buffer after recv: %.1fms (%zu samples)",
498                   buffer_latency_ms, buffer_samples);
499   }
500 
501 #ifdef DEBUG_AUDIO
502   log_debug("Processed %d received audio samples", num_samples);
503 #endif
504 }






References audio_analysis_track_received_sample(),
    AUDIO_BATCH_SAMPLES, audio_ring_buffer_available_read(),
    audio_write_samples(), ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, GET_OPTION,
    log_debug, log_dev_every, log_warn,
    log_warn_every, NS_PER_MS_INT, SET_ERRNO,
    US_PER_MS_INT, and wav_writer_write().






void
  audio_sender_cleanup (void )


Cleanup async audio sender.


Definition at line 386 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


386                                 {
387   // Signal thread to exit
388   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_audio_send_queue_lc)) {
389     atomic_store_bool(&g_audio_sender_should_exit, true);
390     mutex_lock(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex);
391     cond_signal(&g_audio_send_queue_cond);
392     mutex_unlock(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex);
393   }
394 
395   // Thread will be joined by thread_pool_stop_all() in protocol_stop_connection()
396   if (THREAD_IS_CREATED(g_audio_sender_thread_created)) {
397     g_audio_sender_thread_created = false;
398     log_debug("Audio sender thread will be joined by thread pool");
399   }
400 
401   mutex_destroy(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex);
402   cond_destroy(&g_audio_send_queue_cond);
403   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_audio_send_queue_lc);
404 }






References atomic_store_bool(), cond_destroy(),
    cond_signal(), lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), log_debug, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, and THREAD_IS_CREATED.


Referenced by audio_cleanup().






void
  audio_sender_init (void )


Initialize async audio sender queue and thread. Initialize audio
    sender thread.




Uses mutex protection to prevent TOCTOU race conditions where
    multiple threads might attempt initialization simultaneously.




Called from protocol_start_connection() after server
    connection succeeds, to prevent deadlock if connection fails before capture
    thread starts.


Definition at line 344 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


344                              {
345   log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Starting audio sender initialization");
346 
347   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_audio_send_queue_lc, "audio_queue")) {
348     log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Already initialized, returning early");
349     return; // Already initialized
350   }
351 
352   log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Initializing mutex");
353   // Initialize queue structures with error checking
354   if (mutex_init(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex, "audio_send_queue_mutex") != 0) {
355     log_error("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Failed to initialize audio queue mutex");
356     return;
357   }
358 
359   log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Initializing condition variable");
360   if (cond_init(&g_audio_send_queue_cond, "audio_send_queue_cond") != 0) {
361     log_error("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Failed to initialize audio queue condition variable");
362     mutex_destroy(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex);
363     return;
364   }
365 
366   log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Setting queue state");
367   g_audio_send_queue_head = 0;
368   g_audio_send_queue_tail = 0;
369   atomic_store_bool(&g_audio_sender_should_exit, false);
370   atomic_store_bool(&g_audio_sender_exited, false);
371 
372   log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Spawning audio sender thread");
373   // Start sender thread (after lock release to avoid blocking other threads)
374   if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, audio_sender_thread_func, NULL, 5, "audio_sender") == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
375     g_audio_sender_thread_created = true;
376     log_info("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] ✓ Audio sender thread created successfully");
377   } else {
378     log_error("[AUDIO_SENDER_INIT] Failed to spawn audio sender thread in worker pool");
379     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Audio sender thread creation failed");
380   }
381 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_store_bool(),
    cond_init(), g_client_worker_pool, lifecycle_init(),
    LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error, log_info,
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), and
  thread_pool_spawn().


Referenced by protocol_start_connection().






int
  audio_start_thread (void )


Start audio capture thread. Start audio capture thread




Creates and starts the audio capture thread. Also sends stream
    start notification to server.




Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1052 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


1052                          {
1053   log_debug("audio_start_thread called: audio_enabled=%d", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
1054 
1055   // Register audio atomics for debug sync state monitoring (one-time registration)
1056   static bool audio_atomics_registered = false;
1057   if (!audio_atomics_registered) {
1058     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited, "audio_capture_thread_exit_confirmation", NULL);
1059     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_audio_sender_should_exit, "audio_sender_exit_flag", NULL);
1060     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_audio_sender_exited, "audio_sender_exited_confirmation", NULL);
1061     audio_atomics_registered = true;
1062   }
1063 
1064   if (!GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
1065     log_debug("Audio is disabled, skipping audio capture thread creation");
1066     return 0; // Audio disabled - not an error
1067   }
1068 
1069   // Check if thread is already running (not just created flag)
1070   if (g_audio_capture_thread_created && !atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited)) {
1071     log_warn("Audio capture thread already running");
1072     return 0;
1073   }
1074 
1075   // If thread exited, allow recreation
1076   if (g_audio_capture_thread_created && atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited)) {
1077     log_debug("Previous audio capture thread exited, recreating");
1078     // Use timeout to prevent indefinite blocking
1079     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_audio_capture_thread, NULL, 5000 * NS_PER_MS_INT);
1080     if (join_result != 0) {
1081       log_warn("Audio capture thread join timed out after 5s - thread may be deadlocked, "
1082                "forcing thread handle reset (stuck thread resources will not be cleaned up)");
1083       // Thread is stuck - we can't safely reuse the handle, but we can reset our tracking
1084       // This is a resource leak of the stuck thread but continuing is safer than hanging
1085     }
1086     g_audio_capture_thread_created = false;
1087   }
1088 
1089   // Note: STREAM_START for audio was already sent by protocol_start_connection()
1090   // No need to send it again here - the protocol layer handles sending combined stream types
1091 
1092   // Start audio capture thread
1093   atomic_store_bool(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited, false);
1094   if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, audio_capture_thread_func, NULL, 4, "audio_capture") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1095     log_error("Failed to spawn audio capture thread in worker pool");
1096     LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Audio capture thread creation failed");
1097     return -1;
1098   }
1099 
1100   g_audio_capture_thread_created = true;
1101 
1102   return 0;
1103 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_thread_join_timeout(), atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_store_bool(), g_client_worker_pool, GET_OPTION,
    log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET, log_error,
    log_warn, NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, NS_PER_MS_INT, and
    thread_pool_spawn().


Referenced by protocol_start_connection().






void
  audio_stop_thread (void )


Stop audio capture thread. Stop audio capture thread




Gracefully stops the audio capture thread and cleans up resources.
    Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 1113 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


1113                          {
1114   // If audio was never initialized (e.g., in snapshot mode), nothing to stop
1115   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_audio_client_init_lc)) {
1116     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Audio never initialized, nothing to stop");
1117     return;
1118   }
1119 
1120   // Ensure only one thread performs cleanup - prevent double-join and double-cleanup
1121   // First, initialize the lifecycle if it hasn't been yet (moves UNINITIALIZED → INITIALIZED)
1122   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_audio_stop_thread_lc)) {
1123     if (!lifecycle_init(&g_audio_stop_thread_lc, "audio_stop_thread")) {
1124       // Already initialized by another thread or being destroyed
1125       log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Lifecycle already initialized by another thread");
1126       return;
1127     }
1128     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Initialized stop thread lifecycle");
1129   }
1130 
1131   // Now destroy it (moves INITIALIZED → DESTROYING)
1132   if (!lifecycle_destroy_once(&g_audio_stop_thread_lc)) {
1133     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] lifecycle_destroy_once() returned false - already destroying/destroyed");
1134     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Audio stop already in progress or completed, skipping");
1135     return;
1136   }
1137   log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] lifecycle_destroy_once() returned true, proceeding with audio stop");
1138 
1139   // Signal audio worker thread to exit first (stop processing)
1140   log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Signaling audio worker thread to stop");
1141   atomic_store_bool(&g_audio_context.worker_should_stop, true);
1142   // Wake up the worker thread if it's waiting on condition variable
1143   mutex_lock(&g_audio_context.worker_mutex);
1144   cond_signal(&g_audio_context.worker_cond);
1145   mutex_unlock(&g_audio_context.worker_mutex);
1146   log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Worker thread stop signal sent");
1147 
1148   // Signal audio sender thread to exit first.
1149   // This must happen before thread_pool_stop_all() is called, otherwise the sender
1150   // thread will be stuck in cond_wait() and thread_pool_stop_all() will hang forever.
1151   // The sender thread uses a condition variable to wait for packets - we must wake it up.
1152   // NOTE: Only do this if audio_sender_init() was actually called
1153   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_audio_send_queue_lc)) {
1154     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Signaling audio sender thread to exit");
1155     atomic_store_bool(&g_audio_sender_should_exit, true);
1156     mutex_lock(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex);
1157     cond_signal(&g_audio_send_queue_cond);
1158     mutex_unlock(&g_audio_send_queue_mutex);
1159     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Signal sent to sender thread");
1160 
1161     // Wait for audio sender thread to exit gracefully
1162     // This must complete before thread_pool_stop_all() to prevent deadlock
1163     // CRITICAL: Longer timeout to ensure thread completes any in-flight send operations
1164     // Network operations can block for 10-50ms, so we need to wait longer
1165     int wait_count = 0;
1166     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Waiting for sender thread to exit, max 500ms");
1167     while (wait_count < 50 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_sender_exited)) {
1168       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms * 50 = 500ms max wait
1169       wait_count++;
1170     }
1171 
1172     if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_sender_exited)) {
1173       log_warn("[AUDIO_STOP] Audio sender thread not responding after 500ms - will be joined by thread pool");
1174     } else {
1175       log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Sender thread exited successfully");
1176     }
1177   } else {
1178     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Audio sender was never initialized (connection failed), skipping sender shutdown");
1179   }
1180 
1181   log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Checking if capture thread was created: g_audio_capture_thread_created=%d",
1182             g_audio_capture_thread_created);
1183   if (!THREAD_IS_CREATED(g_audio_capture_thread_created)) {
1184     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Capture thread was not created, committing lifecycle and returning");
1185     lifecycle_destroy_commit(&g_audio_stop_thread_lc);
1186     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Lifecycle committed, returning");
1187     return;
1188   }
1189   log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Capture thread was created, waiting for it to exit");
1190 
1191   // Note: We don't call signal_exit() here because that's for global shutdown only
1192   // The audio capture thread checks server_connection_is_active() to detect connection loss
1193 
1194   // Wait for thread to exit gracefully
1195   // Threads check should_exit() every 1-5ms, so timeout can be much shorter than 500ms
1196   int wait_count2 = 0;
1197   log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Waiting for capture thread to exit, max 50ms");
1198   while (wait_count2 < 5 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited)) {
1199     platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms * 5 = 50ms max wait (threads respond in ~1-5ms)
1200     wait_count2++;
1201   }
1202 
1203   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited)) {
1204     log_warn("[AUDIO_STOP] Audio capture thread not responding - will be joined by thread pool");
1205   } else {
1206     log_debug("[AUDIO_STOP] Capture thread exited successfully");
1207   }
1208 
1209   // Thread will be joined by thread_pool_stop_all() in protocol_stop_connection()
1210   g_audio_capture_thread_created = false;
1211 
1212   lifecycle_destroy_commit(&g_audio_stop_thread_lc);
1213 
1214   log_debug("Audio capture thread stopped");
1215 }






References atomic_load_bool(), atomic_store_bool(),
    cond_signal(), lifecycle_destroy_commit(),
    lifecycle_destroy_once(), lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), log_debug, log_warn,
    mutex_lock, mutex_unlock, platform_sleep_us(),
    THREAD_IS_CREATED, and US_PER_MS_INT.


Referenced by audio_cleanup(),
    protocol_stop_connection(), and session_client_like_run().






bool audio_thread_exited (void )


Check if audio capture thread has exited. Check if audio capture
    thread has exited




Returns


true if thread has exited, false otherwise


Definition at line 1224 of file
  src/client/audio.c.


1224                            {
1225   return atomic_load_bool(&g_audio_capture_thread_exited);
1226 }
